name: Marshall Islands

colors.symbolism: the Pacific Ocean which surrounds the islands
courage, the Ralik (sunset) island chain, and the setting sun
peace, the Ratak (sunrise) chain, and the brightness of the morning; also used for the star

description: A blue field crossed diagonally from the lower hoist to the upper fly by two stripes of equal width — orange below, white above — and bearing a single large white twenty-four-pointed star in the upper hoist.
history: Adopted on 1 May 1979, the day the Marshall Islands established self-government as a republic; it was retained when the country became fully independent in association with the United States in 1986. The flag was designed by Emlain Kabua, then-First Lady of the Marshall Islands.

features.symbolism: a deep blue field with two diagonal bands of equal width — orange below, white above — running from the lower hoist to the upper fly; the white stripe represents the Ratak (sunrise) island chain and the orange the Ralik (sunset) chain, together suggesting the country's position just north of the equator and the rising and setting paths of the sun

the twenty-four electoral districts of the Marshall Islands; the four longer rays form a Christian cross signifying the predominant religion; the star's position in the upper hoist marks the country's position in the northern Pacific
